sourceMovie.title: Kiss the Girls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Kiss the Girls (1997) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Gary Fleder's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kiss the Girls clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Gone Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kiss the Girls clicked for you, Gone Girl (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kiss the Girls?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Gone Girl, The Silence of the Lambs—each shares DNA with Kiss the Girls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kiss the Girls?
faq.answer: Kiss the Girls (1997) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Mystery, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kiss the Girls part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Alex Cross Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
